entry.title: Threat Intelligence Has a Human-Shaped Blind Spot
entry.content:
How I realized what I was taught to about threat intelligence was missing something crucial.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Critical Grandstream VoIP Bug Highlights SMB Security Blind Spot
entry.content:
CVE-2026-2329 allows unauthenticated root-level access to SMB phone infrastructure, so attackers can intercept calls, commit toll fraud, and impersonate users.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Connected and Compromised: When IoT Devices Turn Into Threats
entry.content:
Reused passwords, a lack of network segmentation, and poor sanitization processes make the Internet of Things' attack surfaces more dangerous.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Abu Dhabi Finance Week Exposed VIP Passport Details
entry.content:
Unprotected cloud data sends the wrong signal at a time when the emirate's trying to attract investors and establish itself as a global financial center.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Best-in-Class 'Starkiller' Phishing Kit Bypasses MFA
entry.content:
A user-friendly PhaaS tool beats standard methods for detecting phishing attacks by live-proxying legitimate login sites.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Supply Chain Attack Secretly Installs OpenClaw for Cline Users
entry.content:
The malicious version of Cline's npm package — 2.3.0 — was downloaded more than 4,000 times before it was removed.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Emerging Chiplet Designs Spark Fresh Cybersecurity Challenges
entry.content:
As scaled-down circuits with limited functions redefine computing for AI systems and autonomous vehicles, their flexibility demands new approaches to safeguard critical infrastructure.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Latin America's Cyber Maturity Lags Threat Landscape
entry.content:
The slower pace of upgrades has the unintended impact of creating a haven for attackers, especially for initial access brokers and ransomware gangs.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Lessons From AI Hacking: Every Model, Every Layer Is Risky
entry.content:
After two years of finding flaws in AI infrastructure, two Wiz researchers advise security pros to worry less about prompt injection and more about vulnerabilities.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: 'God-Like' Attack Machines: AI Agents Ignore Security Policies
entry.content:
Microsoft Copilot recently summarized and leaked user emails; but any AI agent will go above and beyond to complete assigned tasks, even breaking through their carefully designed guardrails.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Attackers Use New Tool to Scan for React2Shell Exposure
entry.content:
Researchers say threat actors wielded the sophisticated — and unfortunately named — toolkit to target high-value networks for React2Shell exploitation.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Venice Security Emerges From Stealth With $33M Funding for Privileged Access Management
entry.content:
Formerly named Valkyrie, the company’s funding includes $25 million raised in a Series A round. The post Venice Security Emerges From Stealth With $33M Funding for Privileged Access Management appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Nearly 1 Million User Records Compromised in Figure Data Breach
entry.content:
The blockchain-based lender has confirmed a data breach after ShinyHunters leaked over 2GB of data allegedly stolen from the company. The post Nearly 1 Million User Records Compromised in Figure Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: French Government Says 1.2 Million Bank Accounts Exposed in Breach
entry.content:
The Ministry of Economy reported discovering unauthorized access to the national bank account registry FICOBA. The post French Government Says 1.2 Million Bank Accounts Exposed in Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: PromptSpy Android Malware Abuses Gemini AI at Runtime for Persistence
entry.content:
The malware leverages Gemini to analyze on-screen elements and ensure that it remains on the device even after a reboot. The post PromptSpy Android Malware Abuses Gemini AI at Runtime for Persistence appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Chip Testing Giant Advantest Hit by Ransomware
entry.content:
The company is investigating whether any customer or employee data was stolen by hackers. The post Chip Testing Giant Advantest Hit by Ransomware appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: FBI: $20 Million Losses Caused by 700 ATM Jackpotting Attacks in 2025
entry.content:
The FBI has confirmed that the Ploutus malware, which has been around for over a decade, is still being used in the wild. The post FBI: $20 Million Losses Caused by 700 ATM Jackpotting Attacks in 2025 appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: BeyondTrust Vulnerability Exploited in Ransomware Attacks
entry.content:
CISA has updated its KEV entry for CVE-2026-1731 to alert organizations of exploitation in ransomware attacks. The post BeyondTrust Vulnerability Exploited in Ransomware Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: In Other News: Ransomware Shuts US Clinics, ICS Vulnerability Surge, European Parliament Bans AI
entry.content:
Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Axonius lays off employees, Abu Dhabi conference data leak, HackerOne addresses AI concerns. The post In Other News: Ransomware Shuts US Clinics, ICS Vulnerability Surge, European Parliament Bans AI appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Critical Grandstream Phone Vulnerability Exposes Calls to Interception
entry.content:
The flaw tracked as CVE-2026-2329 can be exploited without authentication for remote code execution with root privileges. The post Critical Grandstream Phone Vulnerability Exposes Calls to Interception appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Hackers breach contractor linked to Ukraine’s central bank collectible coin store
entry.content:
The National Bank of Ukraine announced that attackers may have gained access to users’ personal data, including names, phone numbers, email addresses and delivery addresses, in a recent cyberattack.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Russia stepping up hybrid attacks, preparing for long standoff with West, Dutch intelligence warns
entry.content:
Russia’s intensifying cyberattacks, sabotage and covert influence operations across Europe show the Kremlin is preparing for a prolonged confrontation with the West, Dutch intelligence agencies said.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Romanian hacker faces up to 7 years for breaching Oregon emergency management department
entry.content:
In court on Thursday, Catalin Dragomir pleaded guilty to obtaining information from a protected computer and one count of aggravated identity theft.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Happy 16th Birthday, KrebsOnSecurity.com!
entry.content:
KrebsOnSecurity.com celebrates its 16th anniversary today! A huge "thank you" to all of our readers -- newcomers, long-timers and drive-by critics alike. Your engagement this past year here has been tremendous and truly a salve on a handful of dark days. Happily, comeuppance was a strong theme running through our coverage in 2025, with a primary focus on entities that enabled complex and globally-dispersed cybercrime services.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
entry.content:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Loan applications, drivers licences, personal data of 440k Aussies exposed after hacker hits Sydney finance tech company youX
entry.content:
Emma Kirk reports: A hacker has exposed personal data belonging to hundreds of thousands of Australians it allegedly stole from a Sydney finance technology company. Finance platform youX confirmed its systems were accessed by an unauthorised third party during a cyber security incident last week. […] A hacker has claimed to have stolen the personal... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: San Jose slow to tell workers about data breach
entry.content:
When was the last time we read about a breach involving a lost or stolen USB drive? It’s been a while, but now Keith Menconi reports: San Jose administrators have disclosed that private information for current and former city employees may have been compromised, following a data breach last month. The incident occurred on Jan.... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: A single compromised account gave hackers access to 1.2 million French banking records
entry.content:
Emma Woollacott reports: Credentials stolen from a single government official enabled threat actors to access a French national database containing data on more than 1.2 million bank accounts. The attackers were able to access the Fichier des Comptes Bancaires et Assimilés (FICOBA) database, which contains files on all bank accounts opened in France. Stolen credentials... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Romanian hacker faces up to 7 years for breaching Oregon emergency management department
entry.content:
Jonathan Greig reports: A 45-year-old Romanian national pleaded guilty this week to hacking into computers at Oregon’s Department of Emergency Management in June 2021 and selling the access he obtained for $3,000 worth of Bitcoin. Catalin Dragomir also hacked into 10 other U.S. companies, causing financial losses of at least $250,000. He was arrested in... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Resource: Privacy Law Directory — Codamail
entry.content:
Regular readers of my companion privacy-oriented site, PogoWasRight.org, may recall that the site recently noted The Data Broker Directory: Who has your data, where they got it, and who they sell it to by Codamail’s Stephen K. Gielda of Packetderm. Instead of taking a well-deserved break after all the work he did to compile that... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Hospitals at Risk of BeyondTrust Ransomware Hacks
entry.content:
Marianne Kolbasuk McGee reports: U.S. federal authorities and industry officials are urging hospitals and clinics to address a critical flaw in BeyondTrust Remote Support and Privileged Remote Access software, which if exploited, could give an attacker a foothold inside a corporate network. The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services in an alert Thursday warned... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Update Chrome now: Zero-day bug allows code execution via malicious webpages
entry.content:
Pieter Arntz reports: Google has issued a patch for a high‑severity Chrome zero‑day, tracked as CVE‑2026‑2441, a memory bug in how the browser handles certain font features that attackers are already exploiting. CVE-2026-2441 has the questionable honor of being the first Chrome zero-day of 2026. Google considered it serious enough to issue a separate update of the stable... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Discord’s age verification data has a frontend leak — now what?
entry.content:
Interesting Engineering reports: A newly uncovered flaw in Discord’s age verification rollout has added fresh pressure to the company’s 2026 compliance plans. Security researchers recently found that frontend components tied to identity vendor Persona were accessible on the open web, prompting debate over how securely the platform handles sensitive age checks. The discovery surfaced on... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: This is how you do it: Dentist speaks out after practice hit by cyber attack
entry.content:
This could be the smallest breach DataBreaches has reported recently, and yet we are covering it instead of other, much bigger breaches that will undoubtedly generate lots of headlines. Why? Because it represents a refreshing example of quick response and transparency. Dr. Joe McEnhill, owner of Grange Dental Care, said the breach occurred on Thursday... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Ukrainian hackers uncover how Russian drone operators are using Belarus
entry.content:
As seen on InformNapalm: On February 18, 2026, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy imposed sanctions against Belarusian dictator Alyaksandr Lukashenka for his role in escalating and prolonging Russia’s aggressive war against Ukraine. One of the stated reasons is that in the second half of 2025, Russia deployed a system of repeaters for the control of attack... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Managed Care Advisors / Sedgwick Notify Patients of Ransomware Attack
entry.content:
Managed Care Advisors and Sedgwick Government Solutions recently announced a cybersecurity incident involving unauthorized access to a corporate Secure File […] The post Managed Care Advisors / Sedgwick Notify Patients of Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Academic Urology & Urogynecology of Arizona Data Breach Affects 73K Patients
entry.content:
Academic Urology & Urogynecology of Arizona, a division of Palo Verde Hematology and Oncology that serves patients throughout Arizona, has […] The post Academic Urology & Urogynecology of Arizona Data Breach Affects 73K Patients appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: March 1, 2026: Small Healthcare Data Breach HIPAA Reporting Deadline
entry.content:
Healthcare data breaches discovered in calendar year 2025 that affected fewer than 500 individuals must be reported to the HHS’ […] The post March 1, 2026: Small Healthcare Data Breach HIPAA Reporting Deadline appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Three Healthcare Providers Affected by Ransomware Attacks
entry.content:
Issaqueena Pediatric Dentistry in South Carolina, Enhabit Home Health & Hospice in Texas, and AltaMed Health Services in California have […] The post Three Healthcare Providers Affected by Ransomware Attacks appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Data Shows Elevenfold Increase in Data-only Extortion Attacks
entry.content:
There has been a sharp increase in data-only extortion incidents, with ransomware gangs increasingly opting not to encrypt files, instead […] The post Data Shows Elevenfold Increase in Data-only Extortion Attacks appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Top of the World Treatment Center Settles Alleged Risk Analysis HIPAA Violation
entry.content:
The Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) Office for Civil Rights (OCR) has announced its first financial penalty of […] The post Top of the World Treatment Center Settles Alleged Risk Analysis HIPAA Violation appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Cyberattacks Announced by WIRX Pharmacy and Emanuel Medical Center
entry.content:
WIRX Pharmacy in Pennsylvania has experienced a security incident that exposed the protected health information of more than 20,000 current […] The post Cyberattacks Announced by WIRX Pharmacy and Emanuel Medical Center appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Granite Wellness Centers & Pediatric Home Service Settle Class Action Data Breach Lawsuits
entry.content:
Granite Wellness Centers in California and Pediatric Home Service in Minnesota have both settled lawsuits stemming from cyberattacks that exposed […] The post Granite Wellness Centers & Pediatric Home Service Settle Class Action Data Breach Lawsuits appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: UMMC Shuts Clinics While it Grapples with Ransomware Attack
entry.content:
University of Mississippi Medical Center (UMMC) has temporarily closed most of its clinics following a ransomware attack, and scheduled appointments […] The post UMMC Shuts Clinics While it Grapples with Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: FBI: Over $20 million stolen in surge of ATM malware attacks in 2025
entry.content:
The FBI warned that Americans lost more than $20 million last year amid a massive surge in ATM "jackpotting" attacks, in which criminals use malware to force cash machines to dispense money. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Mississippi medical center closes all clinics after ransomware attack
entry.content:
The University of Mississippi Medical Center (UMMC) closed all its clinic locations statewide on Thursday following a ransomware attack. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: PayPal discloses data breach that exposed user info for 6 months
entry.content:
PayPal is notifying customers of a data breach after a software error in a loan application exposed their sensitive personal information, including Social Security numbers, for nearly 6 months last year. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Data breach at French bank registry impacts 1.2 million accounts
entry.content:
The French Ministry of Finance has published an announcement informing of a cybersecurity incident that has impacted 1.2 million accounts. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: CISA: BeyondTrust RCE flaw now exploited in ransomware attacks
entry.content:
Hackers are actively exploiting the CVE-2026-1731 vulnerability in the BeyondTrust Remote Support product, the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) warns. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Japanese tech giant Advantest hit by ransomware attack
entry.content:
Advantest Corporation disclosed that its corporate network has been targeted in a ransomware attack that may have affected customer or employee data. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Amazon: AI-assisted hacker breached 600 Fortinet firewalls in 5 weeks
entry.content:
Amazon is warning that a Russian-speaking hacker used multiple generative AI services as part of a campaign that breached more than 600 FortiGate firewalls across 55 countries in five weeks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.content:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach
